summary: Converged the repo-managed Dozzle hub on docker-runtime-lv3, joined the docker-build-lv3 and monitoring-lv3 agents over the guest network, updated the Proxmox guest firewall policy for agent reachability, and published logs.lv3.org through the shared Keycloak-authenticated NGINX edge.

verification.observed: The final production Dozzle playbook converge completed successfully across proxmox_florin, nginx-lv3, docker-runtime-lv3, docker-build-lv3, and monitoring-lv3 with no failed tasks after the Proxmox guest firewall policy was brought under the same workflow.

verification.check: Private hub health and remote agent reachability
verification.observed: Controller-routed SSH verification over the Proxmox jump path reached docker-runtime-lv3, `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8089/healthcheck` exited successfully, and `sudo docker exec dozzle /dozzle agent-test 10.10.10.30:7007` plus `10.10.10.40:7007` both connected successfully.
`curl -Ik --resolve logs.lv3.org:443:65.108.75.123 https://logs.lv3.org/` returned `HTTP/2 302` to `/oauth2/sign_in` with the expected shared edge security headers.

`uv run --with pytest python -m pytest tests/test_dozzle_runtime_role.py tests/test_nginx_edge_publication_role.py tests/test_post_verify_tasks.py tests/test_dozzle_playbook.py -q` passed with `18 passed`, and `make syntax-check-dozzle`, `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py --validate`, `./scripts/validate_repo.sh health-probes`, and `./scripts/validate_repo.sh alert-rules` all passed.

notes: The verified controller path for private guest checks used a local SSH ProxyCommand through 100.64.0.1 because a direct ad-hoc Ansible invocation did not inherit the jump-host key path cleanly from this workstation session.

The Dozzle rollout required the playbook to converge the Proxmox guest firewall policy as part of the service workflow so remote agent traffic from docker-runtime-lv3 to ports 7007 on docker-build-lv3 and monitoring-lv3 is enforced from the repo.
